diagrams.title: Cloud Service Models Hierarchy
diagrams.description: Shows IaaS, PaaS, SaaS hierarchy from most user control to least control with examples
elements: IaaS (bottom - most control) - AWS EC2, Azure VM
Physical infrastructure at base, Applications at top
diagrams.purpose: Understand the trade-off between control and management responsibility

diagrams.title: Auto-Scaling Process Flow
diagrams.description: Shows how auto-scaling monitors metrics and adjusts instances
elements: Monitoring system collects CPU, Memory, Network metrics
diagrams.purpose: Understand automated scaling mechanism in cloud

diagrams.title: Cloud Bursting Architecture
diagrams.description: Shows workloads spilling from private to public cloud during peak
elements: Peak traffic routes to public cloud
Traffic subsides - returns to private cloud
diagrams.purpose: Visualize hybrid scaling approach for peak handling

diagrams.title: Cloud Capacity Planning Lifecycle
diagrams.description: Shows the continuous process of capacity planning in cloud
elements: Demand forecasting -> Resource provisioning -> Monitoring -> Analysis -> Optimization -> Repeat
Key activities: Right-sizing, Cost monitoring, Performance tuning, Scalability planning
diagrams.purpose: Understand iterative nature of cloud capacity planning
